name: square
description: Interactive coaching course for square compiled in real-time from webcam footage.

steps.name: Drawing the Base Line
steps.description: Begin by placing the marker or chalk on the surface and drawing a straight, horizontal base line to establish the bottom edge of the square.

checklist.description: Verify that the base line is perfectly horizontal and of the desired length.

failure_conditions.trigger_description: The line is slanted or wavy instead of straight.
failure_conditions.mitigation_instruction: Use a straight edge or ruler as a guide to ensure a perfectly straight horizontal line.

steps.name: Drawing the Perpendicular Side
steps.description: Draw a vertical line of the exact same length (4 cm) starting from one end of the base line, ensuring it is perfectly perpendicular (at a 90-degree angle) to the base line.

checklist.description: Verify that the angle between the base line and the new vertical line is exactly 90 degrees.

failure_conditions.trigger_description: The vertical line is tilted or slanted, making the angle unequal to 90 degrees.
failure_conditions.mitigation_instruction: If the line is slanted, erase it and use a right-angle ruler or alignment tool to guide your marker straight up.

steps.name: Completing the Square and Labeling Dimensions
steps.description: Draw the remaining parallel and perpendicular sides to complete the closed four-sided square shape, then label the side lengths to verify equal dimensions.

checklist.description: Verify that all four sides are closed, of equal length, and the corners form 90-degree angles, with the dimension clearly labeled.

failure_conditions.trigger_description: The opposite sides are not parallel, resulting in an irregular quadrilateral instead of a perfect square.
failure_conditions.mitigation_instruction: Use a ruler or measuring scale to ensure opposite sides are of identical length and parallel to each other before finalizing the lines.
